primary_accounting_issue: Appréciation, au regard d’IFRS 10, de la suffisance des droits détenus par un investisseur pour lui conférer le pouvoir de diriger les activités pertinentes d’une entité dans l’analyse du contrôle.

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I selected IFRS 10 because it directly governs the control assessment for an investee, and under the precedence rule no other document in the provided set addresses that same control-of-an-investee issue at an overlapping governing level.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low — the question is squarely about control over an investee, which is directly addressed by IFRS 10.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern how to assess whether an investor's rights are sufficient to give power over the investee's relevant activities.

supporting_authority.reason: These materials clarify reassessment and protective-rights questions that constrain how sufficiency of rights is analysed.

peripheral_authority.reason: This document addresses control for revenue recognition and principal-versus-agent in customer contracts, not control of an investee under IFRS 10.
This document addresses significant influence and the equity method rather than control of an investee.
This document addresses related party disclosures, not the control assessment.

treatment_families.family: Power conveyed through majority voting rights
Power conveyed through less-than-majority voting rights when rights are still sufficient in practice
Power conveyed through contractual or other decision-making rights where voting rights are not the dominant factor
Power conveyed through substantive potential voting rights

approaches.label: Majority voting rights
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 10 identifies majority voting rights as a direct top-level route to power when those rights are substantive and give the current ability to direct relevant activities.

approaches.label: De facto control through voting rights
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 10 recognises that an investor can have power with less than a majority of voting rights when its holding and surrounding facts give it the practical ability to direct relevant activities unilaterally.

approaches.label: Contractual power over relevant activities
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 10 provides a distinct control model for situations in which voting rights are not dominant and power arises from contractual or other decision-making rights.

approaches.label: Substantive potential voting rights
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 10 treats substantive potential voting rights as a separate route by which an investor may obtain the current ability to direct relevant activities.
